fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: ipv4addr
fields.description: Configures the IPv4 address for this A record. Users can dynamically allocate ipv4 address to A record by passing dictionary containing, I(nios_next_ip) and I(CIDR network range). Also update ipv4 address statically to A record by passing dictionary containing, I(old_ipv4addr) and I(new_ipv4addr). See example.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system. Users can also update the name as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Configures the TTL to be associated with this A record.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this A record with.  The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: ipv6addr
fields.description: Configures the IPv6 address for this AAAA record.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system. User can also update the name as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Configures the TTL to be associated with this AAAA record.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this AAAA record with.  The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

fields.label: admin groups
fields.description: The names of the Admin Groups to which this Admin User belongs. Currently, this is limited to only one Admin Group.

fields.label: auth method
fields.description: Authentication method for the admin user.

fields.label: auth type
fields.description: Authentication type for the admin user.

fields.label: ca certificate issuer
fields.description: The CA certificate that is used for user lookup during authentication.

fields.label: client certificate serial number
fields.description: The serial number of the client certificate.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: disable
fields.description: Determines whether the admin user is disabled or not. When this is set to False, the admin user is enabled.

fields.label: email
fields.description: Email address of the admin user.

fields.label: enable certificate authentication
fields.description: Determines whether the user is allowed to log in only with the certificate. Regular username/password authentication will be disabled for this user.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the adminuser name to add or remove from the system. Users can also update the name as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: time zone
fields.description: The time zone for this admin user.

fields.label: use ssh keys
fields.description: Enable/disable the ssh keypair authentication.

fields.label: use time zone
fields.description: Use flag for I(time_zone).

fields.label: canonical
fields.description: Configures the canonical name for this CNAME record.

fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Configures the TTL to be associated with this CNAME record.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this CNAME record with.  The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system. User can also update the hostname as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: network view
fields.description: Specifies the name of the network view to assign the configured DNS view to.  The network view must already be configured on the target system.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: auth zones
fields.description: List of linked authoritative zones. When using I(auth_zones), you must specify at least one I(patterns). Each zone can be specified as a string (FQDN) or as a dict with 'fqdn' and optional 'view' keys. When provided as a string, the first matching zone in any view will be used. When zones with the same name exist in different views, use the dict format to specify which view to use. Example ['example.com'] or [{'fqdn': 'example.com', 'view': 'custom'}]

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: lb method
fields.description: Configures the load balancing method. Used to select pool.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the display name of the DTC LBDN, not DNS related.

fields.label: patterns
fields.description: Specify LBDN wildcards for pattern match.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: topology
fields.description: Configures the topology rules for the C(TOPOLOGY) load balancing method. Required only when I(lb_method) is set to C(TOPOLOGY).

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: The Time To Live (TTL) value for the DTC LBDN. A 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the duration, in seconds, for which the record is valid (cached). Zero indicates that the record should not be cached.

fields.label: types
fields.description: Specifies the list of resource record types supported by LBDN. This option will work properly only if you set the C(wapi_version) variable on your C(provider) variable to a number higher than "2.6".

modules.label: nios_dtc_monitor_http
modules.tooltip: Configure Infoblox NIOS DTC HTTP monitors

fields.label: ciphers
fields.description: Configures an optional cipher list for the secure HTTP/S connection.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: Configures an optional client certificate, supplied in a secure HTTP/S mode if present.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: content check
fields.description: Configures the content check type

fields.label: content check input
fields.description: Configures the portion of the response to use as input for content check.

fields.label: content check op
fields.description: Configures the content check success criteria operator.

fields.label: content check regex
fields.description: Configures the content check regular expression. Values with leading or trailing white space are not valid for this field.

fields.label: content extract group
fields.description: Configures the content extraction sub-expression to extract.

fields.label: content extract type
fields.description: Configures the content extraction expected type for the extracted data.

fields.label: content extract value
fields.description: Configures the content extraction value to compare with the extracted result. Values with leading or trailing white space are not valid for this field.

fields.label: enable sni
fields.description: Configures whether or not Server Name Indication (SNI) for the HTTPS monitor is enabled.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: interval
fields.description: Configures the interval for HTTP health check.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Configures the display name for this DTC monitor. Values with leading or trailing white space are not valid for this field.

fields.label: request
fields.description: Configures the HTTP request to send

fields.label: result
fields.description: Configures the type of the expected result

fields.label: result code
fields.description: Configures the expected return code

fields.label: retry down
fields.description: Configures the value of how many times the server should appear as down to be treated as dead after it was alive.

fields.label: retry up
fields.description: Configures the value of how many times the server should appear as up to be treated as alive after it was dead.

fields.label: secure
fields.description: Configures the security status of the connection.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: validate cert
fields.description: Configures whether the validation of the remote server's certificate is enabled.

modules.label: nios_extensible_attribute
modules.tooltip: Configure Infoblox NIOS extensible attribute definition

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object. The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: default value
fields.description: Configures the default value which is pre populated in the GUI when this attribute is used. Email, URL and string types the value is a with a maximum of 256 characters.

fields.label: flags
fields.description: This field contains extensible attribute flags. The possible values are (A)udited, (C)loud API, Cloud (G)master, (I)nheritable, (L)isted, (M)andatory value, MGM (P)rivate, (R)ead Only, (S)ort enum values, Multiple (V)alues. If there are two or more flags in the field, you must list them according to the order they are listed above. For example, "CR" is a valid value for the "flags" field because C = Cloud API is listed before R = Read only. However, the value "RC" is invalid because the order for the "flags" field is broken.

fields.label: list values
fields.description: Configures a list of preset values associated with the instance of this object. Only applicable when the attribute type is set to ENUM.

fields.label: max
fields.description: Configures the maximum value to be associated with the instance of this object. When provided for an extensible attribute of type STRING the value represents the maximum number of characters the string can contain. When provided for an extensible attribute of type INTEGER the value represents the maximum integer value permitted.Not applicable for other attributes types.

fields.label: min
fields.description: Configures the minimum value to be associated with the instance of this object. When provided for an extensible attribute of type STRING the value represents the minimum number of characters the string can contain. When provided for an extensible attribute of type INTEGER the value represents the minimum integer value permitted. Not applicable for other attributes types.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Configures the intended name of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Configures the intended type for this attribute object definition on the NIOS server.

modules.label: nios_fixed_address
modules.tooltip: Configure Infoblox NIOS DHCP Fixed Address

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: duid
fields.description: The DUID address of the IPv6 interface.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: ipaddr
fields.description: IPV4/V6 address of the fixed address.

fields.label: mac
fields.description: The MAC address of the IPv4 interface. For a fix address reservation specify mac address as 00:00:00:00:00:00

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the hostname with which fixed DHCP ip-address is stored for respective mac.

fields.label: network
fields.description: Specifies the network range in which ipaddr exists.

fields.label: network view
fields.description: Configures the name of the network view to associate with this configured instance.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: aliases
fields.description: Configures an optional list of additional aliases to add to the host record. These are equivalent to CNAMEs but held within a host record. Must be in list format.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: configure for dns
fields.description: Sets the DNS to particular parent. If user needs to bypass DNS user can make the value to false.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system. User can also update the hostname as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Configures the TTL to be associated with this host record.

fields.label: use dns ea inheritance
fields.description: When use_dns_ea_inheritance is True, the EA is inherited from associated zone. The default value is False.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this host record with.  The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: A descriptive comment of the Grid member.

fields.label: config addr type
fields.description: Address configuration type (IPV4/IPV6/BOTH).

fields.label: create token
fields.description: Flag for initiating a create token request for pre-provisioned members.

fields.label: enable ha
fields.description: If set to True, the member has two physical nodes (HA pair).

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Extensible attributes associated with the object.

fields.label: external syslog server enable
fields.description: Determines if external syslog servers should be enabled.

fields.label: host name
fields.description: Specifies the host name of the member to either add or remove from the NIOS instance. Users can also update the name as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: lan2 enabled
fields.description: When set to "true", the LAN2 port is enabled as an independent port or as a port for failover purposes.

fields.label: master candidate
fields.description: Configures the instance of this object to be enabled as a Grid Master Candidate or a regular member node. True enables the member as a Master Candidate

fields.label: platform
fields.description: Configures the Hardware Platform.

fields.label: router id
fields.description: Virtual router identifier. Provide this ID if "ha_enabled" is set to "true". This is a unique VRID number (from 1 to 255) for the local subnet.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: upgrade group
fields.description: The name of the upgrade group to which this Grid member belongs.

fields.label: use syslog proxy setting
fields.description: Use flag for external_syslog_server_enable , syslog_servers, syslog_proxy_setting, syslog_size.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: mail exchanger
fields.description: Configures the mail exchanger FQDN for this MX record.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system. Users can also update the name as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: preference
fields.description: Configures the preference (0-65535) for this MX record.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Configures the TTL to be associated with this host record.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this a record with.  The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: flags
fields.description: Configures the flags field for this NAPTR record. These control the interpretation of the fields for an NAPTR record object. Supported values for the flags field are "U", "S", "P" and "A".

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system.

fields.label: order
fields.description: Configures the order (0-65535) for this NAPTR record. This parameter specifies the order in which the NAPTR rules are applied when multiple rules are present.

fields.label: preference
fields.description: Configures the preference (0-65535) for this NAPTR record. The preference field determines the order NAPTR records are processed when multiple records with the same order parameter are present.

fields.label: regexp
fields.description: Configures the regexp field for this NAPTR record. This is the regular expression-based rewriting rule of the NAPTR record. This should be a POSIX compliant regular expression, including the substitution rule and flags. Refer to RFC 2915 for the field syntax details.

fields.label: replacement
fields.description: Configures the replacement field for this NAPTR record. For nonterminal NAPTR records, this field specifies the next domain name to look up.

fields.label: services
fields.description: Configures the services field (128 characters maximum) for this NAPTR record. The services field contains protocol and service identifiers, such as "http+E2U" or "SIPS+D2T".

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Configures the TTL to be associated with this NAPTR record.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this a record with. The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: container
fields.description: If set to true it'll create the network container to be added or removed from the system.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: network
fields.description: Specifies the network to add or remove from the system.  The value should use CIDR notation.

fields.label: network view
fields.description: Configures the name of the network view to associate with this configured instance.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: template
fields.description: If set on creation, the network is created according to the values specified in the selected template.

fields.label: use logic filter rules
fields.description: If set to true it'll override the logic filter list applied at an upper level.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system. User can also update the hostname as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

modules.label: nios_nsgroup
modules.tooltip: Configure Infoblox DNS Nameserver Authoritative Groups

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: is grid default
fields.description: If set to C(True) this nsgroup will become the default nameserver group for new zones.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the name of the NIOS nameserver group to be managed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: use external primary
fields.description: This flag controls whether the group is using an external primary nameserver. Note that modification of this field requires passing values for I(grid_secondaries) and I(external_primaries).

modules.label: nios_nsgroup_delegation
modules.tooltip: Configure Infoblox DNS Nameserver Delegation Groups

fields.label: extattrs

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the name of the NIOS nameserver group to be managed.

fields.label: state

fields.label: extattrs

modules.label: nios_nsgroup_stubmember
modules.tooltip: Configure Infoblox DNS Nameserver Stub Member Groups

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the name of the NIOS nameserver group to be managed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance. Maximum 256 characters.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: ipv4addr
fields.description: The IPv4 Address of the record. Mutually exclusive with the ipv6addr.

fields.label: ipv6addr
fields.description: The IPv6 Address of the record. Mutually exclusive with the ipv4addr.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the DNS PTR record in FQDN format to add or remove from the system. The field is required only for an PTR object in Forward Mapping Zone.

fields.label: ptrdname
fields.description: The domain name of the DNS PTR record in FQDN format.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Time To Live (TTL) value for the record. A 32-bit unsigned integer that represents the duration, in seconds, that the record is valid (cached). Zero indicates that the record should not be cached.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this a record with. The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

modules.label: nios_range
modules.tooltip: Configure Infoblox NIOS network range object

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: disable
fields.description: Determines whether a range is disabled or not. When this is set to False, the range is enabled.

fields.label: end addr
fields.description: Configures IP address this object instance is to end at. If 'new_end_addr' is defined during a create operation this value is overridden with the value of 'new_end_addr'

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: failover association
fields.description: The name of the DHCP failover association which will be configured to server this object instance. A failover of MS or Nios members can be configured. Can not be configured when 'ms_server' or 'member' are configured.

fields.label: member
fields.description: The hostname of the Nios member which will be configured to server this object instance. Can not be configured when 'ms_server' or 'failover_association' are configured.

fields.label: ms server
fields.description: The hostname of the MS member which will be configured to server this object instance. Can not be configured when 'member' or 'failover_association' are configured.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Congifured the name of the Microsoft scope for the instance of the object on the NIOS server.

fields.label: network
fields.description: Specifies the network to add or remove DHCP range to.  The value should use CIDR notation.

fields.label: network view
fields.description: Configures the name of the network view to associate with this configured instance.

fields.label: new end addr
fields.description: Configures IP address to update this object instance to end at.

fields.label: new start addr
fields.description: Configures IP address to update this object instance to begin from.

fields.label: server association type
fields.description: Configured the type of server association that will be assigned to serve this object instance. This value is not required and will be set as needed automatically during module execution.

fields.label: start addr
fields.description: Configures IP address this object instance is to begin from. If 'new_start_addr' is defined during a create operation this value is overridden with the value of 'new_start_addr'

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: groups
fields.description: The list of the Service Restart Groups to restart.

fields.label: members
fields.description: The list of the Grid Members to restart.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The restart method in case of grid restart.

fields.label: restart option
fields.description: Controls whether services are restarted unconditionally or when needed

fields.label: services
fields.description: The list of services the restart applicable to.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system. Users can also update the name as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Configures the priority (0-65535) for this SRV record.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: target
fields.description: Configures the target FQDN for this SRV record.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Configures the TTL to be associated with this host record.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this a record with.  The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

fields.label: weight
fields.description: Configures the weight (0-65535) for this SRV record.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the fully qualified hostname to add or remove from the system. Users can also update the name as it is possible to pass a dict containing I(new_name), I(old_name). See examples.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: text
fields.description: Text associated with the record. It can contain up to 255 bytes per substring, up to a total of 512 bytes. To enter leading, trailing, or embedded spaces in the text, add quotes around the text to preserve the spaces.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Configures the TTL to be associated with this txt record.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Sets the DNS view to associate this tst record with.  The DNS view must already be configured on the system.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: contact
fields.description: Contact information for person/team managing or using VLAN.

fields.label: department
fields.description: Department where VLAN is used.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description for the VLAN object, may be potentially used for longer VLAN names.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: id
fields.description: Specifies the vlan id to add or remove from the system.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the vlan name to add or remove from the system.

fields.label: parent
fields.description: Specifies the vlan parent to add or remove from the system. Can be either a C(vlanview) or C(vlanrange) name. Fetches the required _ref object automatically. If not specified defaults to vlan view C(default).

fields.label: reserved
fields.description: When set VLAN can only be assigned to IPAM object manually.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Configures a text string comment to be associated with the instance of this object.  The provided text string will be configured on the object instance.

fields.label: extattrs
fields.description: Allows for the configuration of Extensible Attributes on the instance of the object.  This argument accepts a set of key / value pairs for configuration.

fields.label: fqdn
fields.description: Specifies the qualified domain name to either add or remove from the NIOS instance based on the configured C(state) value.

fields.label: ns group
fields.description: Configures the name server group for this zone. Name server group is mutually exclusive with grid primary and grid secondaries.

fields.label: restart if needed
fields.description: If set to true, causes the NIOS DNS service to restart and load the new zone configuration.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Configures the intended state of the instance of the object on the NIOS server.  When this value is set to C(present), the object is configured on the device and when this value is set to C(absent) the value is removed (if necessary) from the device.

fields.label: view
fields.description: Configures the DNS view name for the configured resource.  The specified DNS zone must already exist on the running NIOS instance prior to configuring zones.

fields.label: zone format
fields.description: Create an authorative Reverse-Mapping Zone which is an area of network space for which one or more name servers-primary and secondary-have the responsibility to respond to address-to-name queries. It supports reverse-mapping zones for both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.
